insight.headline: Storefront API Update
insight.what_changed: The Storefront API now supports the @inContext directive with an optional channelId argument, allowing developers to apply a specific sales channel's context to an entire query. This change is available in Storefront API version 2026-10. The @inContext directive enables developers to fetch data specific to a particular sales channel, such as online store or point of sale.
insight.developer_impact: Developers using the Storefront API should update their queries to utilize the @inContext directive with the channelId argument to fetch sales channel-specific data. This change enables more flexibility in querying data and provides a more accurate representation of sales channel-specific information. Developers should review their existing queries and update them to take advantage of this new feature. The Storefront API documentation should be consulted for more information on the @inContext directive and its usage.
insight.merchant_impact: Merchants will benefit from more accurate and sales channel-specific data being displayed in their storefronts, enhancing their overall shopping experience.
insight.why_it_matters: This update provides more flexibility and accuracy in querying sales channel-specific data, enabling developers to build more robust and sales channel-aware applications.

also_worth_knowing.text: Metafield triggers and additional topics are now available for Events
Liquid templates can now compose pages with blocks and partials
Full-stack capabilities to power app analytics

updates.title: Metafield triggers and additional topics are now available for Events
updates.summary: Metafield triggers and additional topics are now available for Events, allowing apps to subscribe to events for various objects and metafield changes.

updates.title: Liquid templates can now compose pages with blocks and partials
updates.summary: Liquid templates can now compose pages with blocks and partials, simplifying theme page building.

updates.title: Full-stack capabilities to power app analytics
updates.summary: Full-stack capabilities are now available to power app analytics, simplifying the process of building merchant-facing analytics.

updates.title: Physical inventory feature preview
updates.summary: The physical inventory feature preview is available, providing early access to new inventory APIs in the unstable version of the GraphQL Admin API.
CLI version 4.5.2 fixes a warning when running theme commands in valid theme directories and bumps Shopify/theme-tools packages.

updates.title: Card deposit endpoint now requires mTLS certificate
updates.summary: The card deposit endpoint now requires a Shopify-issued mTLS client certificate, affecting apps that store cardholder data.

updates.title: @shopify/cli-hydrogen@13.0.2
updates.summary: Hydrogen CLI version 13.0.2 adds non-interactive shop and storefront creation flags and allows bypassing confirmation prompts in hydrogen upgrade.

updates.title: Updated App Store requirements: 4.1.2 Use a unique name for your app
updates.summary: Updated App Store requirements include using a unique name for apps to reduce merchant confusion.

updates.title: Storefront API `@inContext` supports `channelId`
updates.summary: The Storefront API @inContext directive now supports channelId, allowing for channel-specific context in queries.

updates.title: `productId`, `title`,`variantSku`, and `variantTitle` fields added to `ExchangeLineItem`
updates.summary: The ExchangeLineItem type now includes productId, title, variantSku, and variantTitle fields.

updates.title: POS UI Extensions 2026-07 adds discount allocations to bundle components
updates.summary: POS UI Extensions API version 2026-07 adds discount allocations to bundle components, providing more detailed cart line item data.

recap.headline: New features and updates for Shopify developers, including metafield triggers and physical inventory APIs

themes.title: Event-driven development
themes.detail: Metafield triggers and additional topics are now available for Events, allowing apps to subscribe to events for various resources such as Order, Collection, and InventoryItem. This enables more flexible and reactive app development using Webhooks and GraphQL Admin API.

themes.title: Theme development and analytics
themes.detail: Liquid templates can now compose pages with blocks and partials, simplifying theme page building. Additionally, full-stack capabilities are available to power app analytics, reducing the need for external libraries and data warehouses. This is particularly useful for developers using Liquid themes and Polaris.

themes.title: Inventory management and APIs
themes.detail: The physical inventory feature preview introduces a new set of inventory APIs in the unstable version of the GraphQL Admin API, allowing developers to build and test inventory management apps. This is a key feature for developers using the GraphQL Admin API and Hydrogen.

actions.text: Update apps to use new metafield triggers and event topics
Test physical inventory feature preview on a development store
